53.8
CTL
fitness
Chronic Training Load — your rolling 42-day fitness. Higher = fitter. Built from consistent training over weeks.
68.1
ATL
fatigue
Acute Training Load — your rolling 7-day fatigue. Spikes after hard training, drops with rest.
-14.4
TSB
form
Training Stress Balance (CTL − ATL). Positive = fresh, negative = fatigued. Race-ready form is typically 5–25% of your CTL depending on distance and priority.
71.0kg
Weight
7d average
Your 7-day smoothed weight from the most recent data available.
67.9%
RFL (GAP)
vs peak
Relative Fitness Level — your current running fitness as a percentage of your all-time peak. 100% = best ever.
+1.2%
RFL 14d
change
Change in RFL over the last 14 days. Positive = fitness improving. Negative = fitness declining.
-0.6%
Easy RF Gap
vs trend
How your easy runs compare to the overall fitness trend. Negative = easy runs feel harder than expected (possible fatigue or illness).
-%
Age Grade
5k estimate
Age-graded performance — compares your estimated 5K to the world record for your age and sex. 60%+ is good club level, 70%+ is competitive.

Zone split from 30s rolling average of per-second power/HR data where available.
📈 Relative Fitness Level
Blue dots = training, red dots = races, green dashed = easy-run signal.
📈 Relative Fitness Level (14 days)
Current fitness as % of personal best. Trendline projects 7 days ahead with 95% confidence interval.
💪 Training Load
CTL (blue) = fitness. ATL (red) = fatigue. Dashed lines show projection based on planned training sessions.
📅 Volume
Distance over time. W=weeks, M=months, Y=years. Consistency matters more than big weeks.
⚖️ Weight
Weekly average weight (kg) from daily measurements.
🎯 Race Predictions
Solid = predicted. Dashed = adjusted for conditions. ● race ● parkrun.
📊 Age Grade
Dot size = distance. Dark = race, light = parkrun. Red line = trend.
🏃 Recent Runs
Date Activity Dist Pace nPwr HR TSS RFL%
🏆 Top Race Performances
Best races by normalised AG% (adjusted for temperature, terrain, surface, distance).
